operating_state: Total company production reached 1,692 MBOED in Q2 2022, increasing 104 MBOED year-over-year driven by new wells across Lower 48, Alaska, Malaysia and Canada plus Shell Permian acquisition volumes. Average realized price increased to $88.57 per BOE from $50.03 per BOE driven by Brent crude averaging $113.78 per barrel and Henry Hub natural gas averaging $7.17 per MMBTU.
cost_structure: Production and operating expenses increased due to higher volumes from acquisitions and development activities while DD&A expenses decreased from positive price-related reserve revisions. Taxes other than income taxes increased $639 million due to higher commodity prices and volumes, with purchased commodities expenses rising $6.2 billion reflecting higher gas and crude prices. Company allocated $200 million in 2022 capital budget for energy transition activities focused on emissions reduction projects.
material_events: Company increased 2022 expected shareholder distributions to $15 billion from $10 billion, returning $3.3 billion in Q2 through dividend, VROC and share repurchases. Retired $1.8 billion of debt in Q2 progressing toward $5 billion debt reduction target. Signed agreement for 12.5% interest in QatarEnergy's North Field East LNG project and announced non-binding HOA with Sempra for 30% equity in Port Arthur LNG.
macro_exposures: Operations remain exposed to commodity price volatility driven by Russian invasion of Ukraine, sanctions, OPEC supply decisions, global demand fluctuations, inventory levels, inflation and supply chain disruptions. Company maintains unhedged position to retain full upside exposure during higher price periods. Material exposure to evolving climate change legislation and GHG reduction laws across operating jurisdictions creates regulatory compliance uncertainties.
forward_outlook: Q3 2022 production expected at 1.70 to 1.76 MBOED reflecting seasonal turnarounds, with full-year production approximately 1.74 MMBOED. Operating plan capital expenditures guidance unchanged at $7.8 billion excluding acquisition capital. Management committed to three-tier return framework targeting $15 billion total 2022 distributions with net-zero operational emissions by 2050 including 40-50% GHG intensity reduction by 2030.
uncertainty_profile: Port Arthur LNG development subject to concluding definitive agreements, construction contracts, financing and final investment decision. Environmental accrual of $178 million maintained for remediation activities across sites with various legal proceedings ongoing including royalty, tax, environmental and climate change matters. Climate regulatory evolution presents implementation timeline and compliance cost uncertainties that could materially impact operations.
